ESV.1: Six days before the Passover, Jesus therefore came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
ESV.2: So they gave a dinner for him there. Martha served, and Lazarus was one of those reclining with him at table.
ESV.3: Mary therefore took a pound  of expensive ointment made from pure nard, and anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
ESV.4: But Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples (he who was about to betray him), said,
ESV.5: “Why was this ointment not sold for three hundred denarii  and given to the poor?”
ESV.6: He said this, not because he cared about the poor, but because he was a thief, and having charge of the moneybag he used to help himself to what was put into it.
ESV.7: Jesus said, “Leave her alone, so that she may keep it  for the day of my burial.
ESV.8: For the poor you always have with you, but you do not always have me.”
ESV.9: When the large crowd of the Jews learned that Jesus  was there, they came, not only on account of him but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
ESV.10: So the chief priests made plans to put Lazarus to death as well,
ESV.11: because on account of him many of the Jews were going away and believing in Jesus.
ESV.12: The next day the large crowd that had come to the feast heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
ESV.13: So they took branches of palm trees and went out to meet him, crying out, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, even the King of Israel!”
ESV.14: And Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written,
ESV.15: “Fear not, daughter of Zion; behold, your king is coming, sitting on a donkey's colt!”
ESV.16: His disciples did not understand these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things had been written about him and had been done to him.
ESV.17: The crowd that had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead continued to bear witness.
ESV.18: The reason why the crowd went to meet him was that they heard he had done this sign.
ESV.19: So the Pharisees said to one another, “You see that you are gaining nothing. Look, the world has gone after him.”
ESV.20: Now among those who went up to worship at the feast were some Greeks.
ESV.21: So these came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and asked him, “Sir, we wish to see Jesus.”
ESV.22: Philip went and told Andrew; Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
ESV.23: And Jesus answered them,  “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
ESV.24: Truly, truly, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.
ESV.25: Whoever loves his life loses it, and whoever hates his life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
ESV.26: If anyone serves me, he must follow me; and where I am, there will my servant be also. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.
ESV.27: “Now is my soul troubled. And what shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this hour’? But for this purpose I have come to this hour.
ESV.28: Father, glorify your name.” Then a voice came from heaven: “I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.”
ESV.29: The crowd that stood there and heard it said that it had thundered. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”
ESV.30: Jesus answered,  “This voice has come for your sake, not mine.
ESV.31: Now is the judgment of this world; now will the ruler of this world be cast out.
ESV.32: And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”
ESV.33: He said this to show by what kind of death he was going to die.
ESV.34: So the crowd answered him, “We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
ESV.35: So Jesus said to them,  “The light is among you for a little while longer. Walk while you have the light, lest darkness overtake you. The one who walks in the darkness does not know where he is going.
ESV.36: While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become sons of light.” The Unbelief of the People When Jesus had said these things, he departed and hid himself from them.
ESV.37: Though he had done so many signs before them, they still did not believe in him,
ESV.38: so that the word spoken by the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled: “Lord, who has believed what he heard from us, and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
ESV.39: Therefore they could not believe. For again Isaiah said,
ESV.40: “He has blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, lest they see with their eyes, and understand with their heart, and turn, and I would heal them.”
ESV.41: Isaiah said these things because he saw his glory and spoke of him.
ESV.42: Nevertheless, many even of the authorities believed in him, but for fear of the Pharisees they did not confess it, so that they would not be put out of the synagogue;
ESV.43: for they loved the glory that comes from man more than the glory that comes from God.
ESV.44: And Jesus cried out and said,  “Whoever believes in me, believes not in me but in him who sent me.
ESV.45: And whoever sees me sees him who sent me.
ESV.46: I have come into the world as light, so that whoever believes in me may not remain in darkness.
ESV.47: If anyone hears my words and does not keep them, I do not judge him; for I did not come to judge the world but to save the world.
ESV.48: The one who rejects me and does not receive my words has a judge; the word that I have spoken will judge him on the last day.
ESV.49: For I have not spoken on my own authority, but the Father who sent me has himself given me a commandment — what to say and what to speak.
ESV.50: And I know that his commandment is eternal life. What I say, therefore, I say as the Father has told me.”

NLT.1: Six days before the Passover celebration began, Jesus arrived in Bethany, the home of Lazarus — the man he had raised from the dead.
NLT.2: A dinner was prepared in Jesus’ honor. Martha served, and Lazarus was among those who ate with him.
NLT.3: Then Mary took a twelve-ounce jar of expensive perfume made from essence of nard, and she anointed Jesus’ feet with it, wiping his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance.
NLT.4: But Judas Iscariot, the disciple who would soon betray him, said,
NLT.5: “That perfume was worth a year’s wages. It should have been sold and the money given to the poor.”
NLT.6: Not that he cared for the poor — he was a thief, and since he was in charge of the disciples’ money, he often stole some for himself.
NLT.7: Jesus replied, “Leave her alone. She did this in preparation for my burial.
NLT.8: You will always have the poor among you, but you will not always have me.”
NLT.9: When all the people heard of Jesus’ arrival, they flocked to see him and also to see Lazarus, the man Jesus had raised from the dead.
NLT.10: Then the leading priests decided to kill Lazarus, too,
NLT.11: for it was because of him that many of the people had deserted them and believed in Jesus.
NLT.12: The next day, the news that Jesus was on the way to Jerusalem swept through the city. A large crowd of Passover visitors
NLT.13:
took palm branches and went down the road to meet him. They shouted, “Praise God!
Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the LORD!
Hail to the King of Israel!”
NLT.14: Jesus found a young donkey and rode on it, fulfilling the prophecy that said:
NLT.15:
“Don’t be afraid, people of Jerusalem.
Look, your King is coming,
riding on a donkey’s colt.”
NLT.16: His disciples didn’t understand at the time that this was a fulfillment of prophecy. But after Jesus entered into his glory, they remembered what had happened and realized that these things had been written about him.
NLT.17: Many in the crowd had seen Jesus call Lazarus from the tomb, raising him from the dead, and they were telling others about it.
NLT.18: That was the reason so many went out to meet him — because they had heard about this miraculous sign.
NLT.19: Then the Pharisees said to each other, “There’s nothing we can do. Look, everyone has gone after him!”
NLT.20: Some Greeks who had come to Jerusalem for the Passover celebration
NLT.21: paid a visit to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee. They said, “Sir, we want to meet Jesus.”
NLT.22: Philip told Andrew about it, and they went together to ask Jesus.
NLT.23: Jesus replied, “Now the time has come for the Son of Man to enter into his glory.
NLT.24: I tell you the truth, unless a kernel of wheat is planted in the soil and dies, it remains alone. But its death will produce many new kernels — a plentiful harvest of new lives.
NLT.25: Those who love their life in this world will lose it. Those who care nothing for their life in this world will keep it for eternity.
NLT.26: Anyone who wants to serve me must follow me, because my servants must be where I am. And the Father will honor anyone who serves me.
NLT.27: “Now my soul is deeply troubled. Should I pray, ‘Father, save me from this hour’? But this is the very reason I came!
NLT.28: Father, bring glory to your name.” Then a voice spoke from heaven, saying, “I have already brought glory to my name, and I will do so again.”
NLT.29: When the crowd heard the voice, some thought it was thunder, while others declared an angel had spoken to him.
NLT.30: Then Jesus told them, “The voice was for your benefit, not mine.
NLT.31: The time for judging this world has come, when Satan, the ruler of this world, will be cast out.
NLT.32: And when I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to myself.”
NLT.33: He said this to indicate how he was going to die.
NLT.34: The crowd responded, “We understood from Scripture that the Messiah would live forever. How can you say the Son of Man will die? Just who is this Son of Man, anyway?”
NLT.35: Jesus replied, “My light will shine for you just a little longer. Walk in the light while you can, so the darkness will not overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness cannot see where they are going.
NLT.36: Put your trust in the light while there is still time; then you will become children of the light.” After saying these things, Jesus went away and was hidden from them.
NLT.37: But despite all the miraculous signs Jesus had done, most of the people still did not believe in him.
NLT.38:
This is exactly what Isaiah the prophet had predicted: “LORD, who has believed our message?
To whom has the LORD revealed his powerful arm?”
NLT.39: But the people couldn’t believe, for as Isaiah also said,
NLT.40:
“The Lord has blinded their eyes
and hardened their hearts —
so that their eyes cannot see,
and their hearts cannot understand,
and they cannot turn to me
and have me heal them.”
NLT.41: Isaiah was referring to Jesus when he said this, because he saw the future and spoke of the Messiah’s glory.
NLT.42: Many people did believe in him, however, including some of the Jewish leaders. But they wouldn’t admit it for fear that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
NLT.43: For they loved human praise more than the praise of God.
NLT.44: Jesus shouted to the crowds, “If you trust me, you are trusting not only me, but also God who sent me.
NLT.45: For when you see me, you are seeing the one who sent me.
NLT.46: I have come as a light to shine in this dark world, so that all who put their trust in me will no longer remain in the dark.
NLT.47: I will not judge those who hear me but don’t obey me, for I have come to save the world and not to judge it.
NLT.48: But all who reject me and my message will be judged on the day of judgment by the truth I have spoken.
NLT.49: I don’t speak on my own authority. The Father who sent me has commanded me what to say and how to say it.
NLT.50: And I know his commands lead to eternal life; so I say whatever the Father tells me to say.”

WEB.1: Then six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, who had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.
WEB.2: So they made him a supper there. Martha served, but Lazarus was one of those who sat at the table with him.
WEB.3: Mary, therefore, took a pound  of ointment of pure nard, very precious, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the ointment.
WEB.4: Then Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, one of his disciples, who would betray him, said,
WEB.5: “Why wasn’t this ointment sold for three hundred denarii,  and given to the poor?”
WEB.6: Now he said this, not because he cared for the poor, but because he was a thief, and having the money box, used to steal what was put into it.
WEB.7: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She has kept this for the day of my burial.
WEB.8: For you always have the poor with you, but you don’t always have me.”
WEB.9: A large crowd therefore of the Jews learned that he was there, and they came, not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.
WEB.10: But the chief priests conspired to put Lazarus to death also,
WEB.11: because on account of him many of the Jews went away and believed in Jesus.
WEB.12: On the next day a great multitude had come to the feast. When they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
WEB.13: they took the branches of the palm trees, and went out to meet him, and cried out, “Hosanna!  Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord,  the King of Israel!”
WEB.14: Jesus, having found a young donkey, sat on it. As it is written,
WEB.15: “Don’t be afraid, daughter of Zion. Behold, your King comes, sitting on a donkey’s colt.”
WEB.16: His disciples didn’t understand these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written about him, and that they had done these things to him.
WEB.17: The multitude therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb, and raised him from the dead, was testifying about it.
WEB.18: For this cause also the multitude went and met him, because they heard that he had done this sign.
WEB.19: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, “See how you accomplish nothing. Behold, the world has gone after him.”
WEB.20: Now there were certain Greeks among those that went up to worship at the feast.
WEB.21: These, therefore, came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida of Galilee, and asked him, saying, “Sir, we want to see Jesus.”
WEB.22: Philip came and told Andrew, and in turn, Andrew came with Philip, and they told Jesus.
WEB.23: Jesus answered them, “The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
WEB.24: Most certainly I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains by itself alone. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
WEB.25: He who loves his life will lose it. He who hates his life in this world will keep it to eternal life.
WEB.26: If anyone serves me, let him follow me. Where I am, there will my servant also be. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.
WEB.27: “Now my soul is troubled. What shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this time?’ But for this cause I came to this time.
WEB.28: Father, glorify your name!” Then there came a voice out of the sky, saying, “I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.”
WEB.29: The multitude therefore, who stood by and heard it, said that it had thundered. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”
WEB.30: Jesus answered, “This voice hasn’t come for my sake, but for your sakes.
WEB.31: Now is the judgment of this world. Now the prince of this world will be cast out.
WEB.32: And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”
WEB.33: But he said this, signifying by what kind of death he should die.
WEB.34: The multitude answered him, “We have heard out of the law that the Christ remains forever.  How do you say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up?’ Who is this Son of Man?”
WEB.35: Jesus therefore said to them, “Yet a little while the light is with you. Walk while you have the light, that darkness doesn’t overtake you. He who walks in the darkness doesn’t know where he is going.
WEB.36: While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become children of light.” Jesus said these things, and he departed and hid himself from them.
WEB.37: But though he had done so many signs before them, yet they didn’t believe in him,
WEB.38: that the word of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spoke, “Lord, who has believed our report? To whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
WEB.39: For this cause they couldn’t believe, for Isaiah said again,
WEB.40: “He has blinded their eyes and he hardened their heart, lest they should see with their eyes, and perceive with their heart, and would turn, and I would heal them.”
WEB.41: Isaiah said these things when he saw his glory, and spoke of him.
WEB.42: Nevertheless even of the rulers many believed in him, but because of the Pharisees they didn’t confess it, so that they wouldn’t be put out of the synagogue,
WEB.43: for they loved men’s praise more than God’s praise.
WEB.44: Jesus cried out and said, “Whoever believes in me, believes not in me, but in him who sent me.
WEB.45: He who sees me sees him who sent me.
WEB.46: I have come as a light into the world, that whoever believes in me may not remain in the darkness.
WEB.47: If anyone listens to my sayings, and doesn’t believe, I don’t judge him. For I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
WEB.48: He who rejects me, and doesn’t receive my sayings, has one who judges him. The word that I spoke, the same will judge him in the last day.
WEB.49: For I spoke not from myself, but the Father who sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
WEB.50: I know that his commandment is eternal life. The things therefore which I speak, even as the Father has said to me, so I speak.”

AMP.1: Six days before the Passover, Jesus went to Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom He had raised from the dead.
AMP.2: So they gave a supper for Him there. Martha was serving, and Lazarus was one of those reclining at the table with Him.
AMP.3: Then Mary took a pound of very expensive perfume of pure nard, and she poured it on Jesus' feet and wiped His feet with her hair; and the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
AMP.4: But Judas Iscariot, one of His disciples, the one who was going to betray Him, said,
AMP.5: "Why was this perfume not sold for three hundred denarii and [the money] given to the poor?"
AMP.6: Now he said this, not because he cared about the poor [for he had never cared about them], but because he was a thief; and since he had the money box [serving as treasurer for the twelve disciples], he used to pilfer what was put into it.
AMP.7: So Jesus said, "Let her alone, so that she may keep [the rest of] it for the day of My burial.
AMP.8: You always have the poor with you, but you do not always have Me."
AMP.9: A large crowd of Jews learned that He was there [at Bethany]; and they came, not only because of Jesus, but also to see Lazarus, whom He had raised from the dead.
AMP.10: So the chief priests planned to kill Lazarus also,
AMP.11: because on account of him many of the Jews were going away [from the teaching and traditions of the Jewish leaders] and believing in Jesus [following Him as Savior and Messiah].
AMP.12: The next day, when the large crowd who had come to the Passover feast heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
AMP.13: they took branches of palm trees [in homage to Him as King] and went out to meet Him, and they began shouting and kept shouting "Hosanna! Blessed (celebrated, praised) is He who comes in the name of the Lord, even the King of Israel!"
AMP.14: And Jesus, finding a young donkey, sat on it; just as it is written [in Scripture],
AMP.15: "<e>Do not fear, Daughter of Zion; Behold, Your King is coming, seated on a donkey's colt</e>."
AMP.16: His disciples did not understand [the meaning of] these things at first; but when Jesus was glorified and exalted, they remembered that these things had been written about Him and had been done to Him.
AMP.17: So the people, who were with Him when He called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead, continued to tell others about Him.
AMP.18: For this reason the crowd went to meet Him, because they heard that He had performed this [miraculous] sign.
AMP.19: Then the Pharisees [argued and] said to one another, "You see that your efforts are futile. Look! The whole world has gone [running] after Him!"
AMP.20: Now there were some Greeks (Gentiles) among those who were going up to worship at the feast;
AMP.21: these came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, with a request, saying, "Sir, we wish to see Jesus."
AMP.22: Philip came and told Andrew; then Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
AMP.23: And Jesus answered them, "The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified and exalted.
AMP.24: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone [just one grain, never more]. But if it dies, it produces much grain and yields a harvest.
AMP.25: The one who loves his life [eventually] loses it [through death], but the one who hates his life in this world [and is concerned with pleasing God] will keep it for life eternal.
AMP.26: If anyone serves Me, he must [continue to faithfully] follow Me [without hesitation, holding steadfastly to Me, conforming to My example in living and, if need be, suffering or perhaps dying because of faith in Me]; and wherever I am [in heaven's glory], there will My servant be also. If anyone serves Me, the Father will honor him.
AMP.27: "Now My soul is troubled and deeply distressed; what shall I say? 'Father, save Me from this hour [of trial and agony]'? But it is for this [very] purpose that I have come to this hour [this time and place].
AMP.28: [Rather, I will say,] 'Father, glorify (honor, extol) Your name!'" Then a voice came from heaven saying, "I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again."
AMP.29: The crowd of people who stood nearby and heard the voice said that it had thundered; others said, "An angel has spoken to Him!"
AMP.30: Jesus answered, "This voice has come for your sake, not for mine.
AMP.31: Now judgment is upon this world [the sentence is being passed]. Now the ruler of this world (Satan) will be cast out.
AMP.32: And I, if and when I am lifted up from the earth [on the cross], will draw all people to Myself [Gentiles, as well as Jews]."
AMP.33: He said this to indicate the kind of death by which He was to die.
AMP.34: At this the crowd answered Him, " We have heard from the Law that the Christ is to remain forever; how then can You say, 'The Son of Man must be lifted up'? Who is this Son of Man?"
AMP.35: So Jesus said to them, "The Light is among you [only] a little while longer. Walk while you have the Light [keep on living by it], so that darkness will not overtake you. He who walks in the darkness does not know where he is going [he is drifting aimlessly].
AMP.36:
While you have the Light, believe and trust in the Light [have faith in it, hold on to it, rely on it], so that you may become sons of Light [being filled with Light as followers of God]."
Jesus said these things, and then He left and hid Himself from them.
AMP.37: Even though He had done so many signs (attesting miracles) right before them, yet they still did not believe and failed to trust Him—
AMP.38: This was to fulfill what Isaiah the prophet said: "Lord, who has believed our message? And to whom has the arm (the power) of the Lord been shown (unveiled, revealed)?"
AMP.39: Therefore they could not believe, for Isaiah said again,
AMP.40: "He has blinded their eyes and He hardened their heart, to keep them from seeing with their eyes and understanding with their heart and being converted; otherwise, I [their God] would heal them."
AMP.41: Isaiah said these things because he saw His glory and spoke about Him.
AMP.42: Nevertheless, even many of the leading men believed in Him [as Savior and Messiah], but because of the Pharisees they would not confess it, for fear that [if they acknowledged Him openly] they would be put out of the synagogue (excommunicated);
AMP.43: for they loved the approval of men more than the approval of God.
AMP.44: But Jesus loudly declared, "The one who believes and trusts in Me does not believe [only] in Me but [also believes] in Him who sent Me.
AMP.45: And whoever sees Me sees the One who sent Me.
AMP.46: I have come as Light into the world, so that everyone who believes and trusts in Me [as Savior—all those who anchor their hope in Me and rely on the truth of My message] will not continue to live in darkness.
AMP.47: If anyone hears My words and does not keep them, I do not judge him; for I did not come to judge and condemn the world [that is, to initiate the final judgment of the world], but to save the world.
AMP.48: Whoever rejects Me and refuses to accept My teachings, has one who judges him; the very word that I spoke will judge and condemn him on the last day.
AMP.49: For I have never spoken on My own initiative or authority, but the Father Himself who sent Me has given Me a commandment regarding what to say and what to speak.
AMP.50: I know that His commandment is eternal life. So the things I speak, I speak [in accordance with His exact instruction,] just as the Father has told Me."

MSG.1: Six days before Passover, Jesus entered Bethany where Lazarus, so recently raised from the dead, was living.
MSG.2: Lazarus and his sisters invited Jesus to dinner at their home. Martha served. Lazarus was one of those sitting at the table with them.
MSG.3: Mary came in with a jar of very expensive aromatic oils, anointed and massaged Jesus' feet, and then wiped them with her hair. The fragrance of the oils filled the house.
MSG.4: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples, even then getting ready to betray him, said,
MSG.5: "Why wasn't this oil sold and the money given to the poor? It would have easily brought three hundred silver pieces."
MSG.6: He said this not because he cared two cents about the poor but because he was a thief. He was in charge of their common funds, but also embezzled them.
MSG.7: Jesus said, "Let her alone. She's anticipating and honoring the day of my burial.
MSG.8: You always have the poor with you. You don't always have me."
MSG.9: Word got out among the Jews that he was back in town. The people came to take a look, not only at Jesus but also at Lazarus, who had been raised from the dead.
MSG.10: So the high priests plotted to kill Lazarus
MSG.11: because so many of the Jews were going over and believing in Jesus on account of him.
MSG.12: The next day the huge crowd that had arrived for the Feast heard that Jesus was entering Jerusalem.
MSG.13: They broke off palm branches and went out to meet him. And they cheered: Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in God's name! Yes! The King of Israel!
MSG.14: Jesus got a young donkey and rode it, just as the Scripture has it:
MSG.15: No fear, Daughter Zion: See how your king comes, riding a donkey's colt.
MSG.16: The disciples didn't notice the fulfillment of many Scriptures at the time, but after Jesus was glorified, they remembered that what was written about him matched what was done to him.
MSG.17: The crowd that had been with him when he called Lazarus from the tomb, raising him from the dead, was there giving eyewitness accounts.
MSG.18: It was because they had spread the word of this latest God-sign that the crowd swelled to a welcoming parade.
MSG.19: The Pharisees took one look and threw up their hands: "It's out of control. The world's in a stampede after him."
MSG.20: There were some Greeks in town who had come up to worship at the Feast.
MSG.21: They approached Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee: "Sir, we want to see Jesus. Can you help us?"
MSG.22: Philip went and told Andrew. Andrew and Philip together told Jesus.
MSG.23: Jesus answered, "Time's up. The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
MSG.24: "Listen carefully: Unless a grain of wheat is buried in the ground, dead to the world, it is never any more than a grain of wheat. But if it is buried, it sprouts and reproduces itself many times over.
MSG.25: In the same way, anyone who holds on to life just as it is destroys that life. But if you let it go, reckless in your love, you'll have it forever, real and eternal.
MSG.26: "If any of you wants to serve me, then follow me. Then you'll be where I am, ready to serve at a moment's notice. The Father will honor and reward anyone who serves me.
MSG.27: "Right now I am storm-tossed. And what am I going to say? 'Father, get me out of this'? No, this is why I came in the first place.
MSG.28: I'll say, 'Father, put your glory on display.' " A voice came out of the sky: "I have glorified it, and I'll glorify it again."
MSG.29: The listening crowd said, "Thunder!" Others said, "An angel spoke to him!"
MSG.30: Jesus said, "The voice didn't come for me but for you.
MSG.31: At this moment the world is in crisis. Now Satan, the ruler of this world, will be thrown out.
MSG.32: And I, as I am lifted up from the earth, will attract everyone to me and gather them around me."
MSG.33: He put it this way to show how he was going to be put to death.
MSG.34: Voices from the crowd answered, "We heard from God's Law that the Messiah lasts forever. How can it be necessary, as you put it, that the Son of Man 'be lifted up'? Who is this 'Son of Man'?"
MSG.35: Jesus said, "For a brief time still, the light is among you. Walk by the light you have so darkness doesn't destroy you. If you walk in darkness, you don't know where you're going.
MSG.36: As you have the light, believe in the light. Then the light will be within you, and shining through your lives. You'll be children of light." Jesus said all this, and then went into hiding.
MSG.37: All these God-signs he had given them and they still didn't get it, still wouldn't trust him.
MSG.38: This proved that the prophet Isaiah was right: God, who believed what we preached? Who recognized God's arm, outstretched and ready to act?
MSG.39: First they wouldn't believe, then they couldn't—again, just as Isaiah said:
MSG.40: Their eyes are blinded, their hearts are hardened, So that they wouldn't see with their eyes and perceive with their hearts, And turn to me, God, so I could heal them.
MSG.41: Isaiah said these things after he got a glimpse of God's cascading brightness that would pour through the Messiah.
MSG.42: On the other hand, a considerable number from the ranks of the leaders did believe. But because of the Pharisees, they didn't come out in the open with it. They were afraid of getting kicked out of the meeting place.
MSG.43: When push came to shove they cared more for human approval than for God's glory.
MSG.44: Jesus summed it all up when he cried out, "Whoever believes in me, believes not just in me but in the One who sent me.
MSG.45: Whoever looks at me is looking, in fact, at the One who sent me.
MSG.46: I am Light that has come into the world so that all who believe in me won't have to stay any longer in the dark.
MSG.47: "If anyone hears what I am saying and doesn't take it seriously, I don't reject him. I didn't come to reject the world;
MSG.48: I came to save the world. But you need to know that whoever puts me off, refusing to take in what I'm saying, is willfully choosing rejection. The Word, the Word-made-flesh that I have spoken and that I am, that Word and no other is the last word.
MSG.49: I'm not making any of this up on my own. The Father who sent me gave me orders, told me what to say and how to say it.
MSG.50: And I know exactly what his command produces: real and eternal life. That's all I have to say. What the Father told me, I tell you."

NIV.1:
Jesus Anointed at Bethany
Six days before the Passover, Jesus arrived at Bethany, where Lazarus lived, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
NIV.2: Here a dinner was given in Jesus’ honor. Martha served, while Lazarus was among those reclining at the table with him.
NIV.3: Then Mary took about a pint of pure nard, an expensive perfume; she poured it on Jesus’ feet and wiped his feet with her hair. And the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
NIV.4: But one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, who was later to betray him, objected,
NIV.5: “Why wasn’t this perfume sold and the money given to the poor? It was worth a year’s wages.”
NIV.6: He did not say this because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief; as keeper of the money bag, he used to help himself to what was put into it.
NIV.7: “Leave her alone,” Jesus replied. “It was intended that she should save this perfume for the day of my burial.
NIV.8: You will always have the poor among you, but you will not always have me.”
NIV.9: Meanwhile a large crowd of Jews found out that Jesus was there and came, not only because of him but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
NIV.10: So the chief priests made plans to kill Lazarus as well,
NIV.11: for on account of him many of the Jews were going over to Jesus and putting their faith in him.
NIV.12:
The Triumphal Entry
The next day the great crowd that had come for the Feast heard that Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem.
NIV.13:
They took palm branches and went out to meet him, shouting,
“Hosanna!”
“Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!”
“Blessed is the King of Israel!”
NIV.14: Jesus found a young donkey and sat upon it, as it is written,
NIV.15:
“Do not be afraid, O Daughter of Zion;
see, your king is coming,
seated on a donkey’s colt.”
NIV.16: At first his disciples did not understand all this. Only after Jesus was glorified did they realize that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
NIV.17: Now the crowd that was with him when he called Lazarus from the tomb and raised him from the dead continued to spread the word.
NIV.18: Many people, because they had heard that he had given this miraculous sign, went out to meet him.
NIV.19: So the Pharisees said to one another, “See, this is getting us nowhere. Look how the whole world has gone after him!”
NIV.20:
Jesus Predicts His Death
Now there were some Greeks among those who went up to worship at the Feast.
NIV.21: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, with a request. “Sir,” they said, “we would like to see Jesus.”
NIV.22: Philip went to tell Andrew; Andrew and Philip in turn told Jesus.
NIV.23: Jesus replied, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
NIV.24: I tell you the truth, unless a kernel of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed. But if it dies, it produces many seeds.
NIV.25: The man who loves his life will lose it, while the man who hates his life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
NIV.26: Whoever serves me must follow me; and where I am, my servant also will be. My Father will honor the one who serves me.
NIV.27: “Now my heart is troubled, and what shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this hour’? No, it was for this very reason I came to this hour.
NIV.28:
Father, glorify your name!”
Then a voice came from heaven, “I have glorified it, and will glorify it again.”
NIV.29: The crowd that was there and heard it said it had thundered; others said an angel had spoken to him.
NIV.30: Jesus said, “This voice was for your benefit, not mine.
NIV.31: Now is the time for judgment on this world; now the prince of this world will be driven out.
NIV.32: But I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all men to myself.”
NIV.33: He said this to show the kind of death he was going to die.
NIV.34: The crowd spoke up, “We have heard from the Law that the Christ will remain forever, so how can you say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this ‘Son of Man’?”
NIV.35: Then Jesus told them, “You are going to have the light just a little while longer. Walk while you have the light, before darkness overtakes you. The man who walks in the dark does not know where he is going.
NIV.36: Put your trust in the light while you have it, so that you may become sons of light.” When he had finished speaking, Jesus left and hid himself from them.
NIV.37:
The Jews Continue in Their Unbelief
Even after Jesus had done all these miraculous signs in their presence, they still would not believe in him.
NIV.38:
This was to fulfill the word of Isaiah the prophet:
“Lord, who has believed our message
and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
NIV.39: For this reason they could not believe, because, as Isaiah says elsewhere:
NIV.40:
“He has blinded their eyes
and deadened their hearts,
so they can neither see with their eyes,
nor understand with their hearts,
nor turn—and I would heal them.”
NIV.41: Isaiah said this because he saw Jesus’ glory and spoke about him.
NIV.42: Yet at the same time many even among the leaders believed in him. But because of the Pharisees they would not confess their faith for fear they would be put out of the synagogue;
NIV.43: for they loved praise from men more than praise from God.
NIV.44: Then Jesus cried out, “When a man believes in me, he does not believe in me only, but in the one who sent me.
NIV.45: When he looks at me, he sees the one who sent me.
NIV.46: I have come into the world as a light, so that no one who believes in me should stay in darkness.
NIV.47: “As for the person who hears my words but does not keep them, I do not judge him. For I did not come to judge the world, but to save it.
NIV.48: There is a judge for the one who rejects me and does not accept my words; that very word which I spoke will condemn him at the last day.
NIV.49: For I did not speak of my own accord, but the Father who sent me commanded me what to say and how to say it.
NIV.50: I know that his command leads to eternal life. So whatever I say is just what the Father has told me to say.”

NRSVCE.17: So the crowd that had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead continued to testify.
NRSVCE.18: It was also because they heard that he had performed this sign that the crowd went to meet him.
NRSVCE.19: The Pharisees then said to one another, “You see, you can do nothing. Look, the world has gone after him!”
NRSVCE.20: Now among those who went up to worship at the festival were some Greeks.
NRSVCE.21: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and said to him, “Sir, we wish to see Jesus.”
NRSVCE.22: Philip went and told Andrew; then Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
NRSVCE.23: Jesus answered them, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
NRSVCE.24: Very truly, I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains just a single grain; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.
NRSVCE.25: Those who love their life lose it, and those who hate their life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
NRSVCE.26: Whoever serves me must follow me, and where I am, there will my servant be also. Whoever serves me, the Father will honor.
NRSVCE.27: “Now my soul is troubled. And what should I say — ‘Father, save me from this hour’? No, it is for this reason that I have come to this hour.
NRSVCE.28: Father, glorify your name.” Then a voice came from heaven, “I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.”
NRSVCE.29: The crowd standing there heard it and said that it was thunder. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”
NRSVCE.30: Jesus answered, “This voice has come for your sake, not for mine.
NRSVCE.31: Now is the judgment of this world; now the ruler of this world will be driven out.
NRSVCE.32: And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”
NRSVCE.33: He said this to indicate the kind of death he was to die.
NRSVCE.34: The crowd answered him, “We have heard from the law that the Messiah remains forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
NRSVCE.35: Jesus said to them, “The light is with you for a little longer. Walk while you have the light, so that the darkness may not overtake you. If you walk in the darkness, you do not know where you are going.
NRSVCE.36: While you have the light, believe in the light, so that you may become children of light.” The Unbelief of the People After Jesus had said this, he departed and hid from them.
NRSVCE.37: Although he had performed so many signs in their presence, they did not believe in him.
NRSVCE.38:
This was to fulfill the word spoken by the prophet Isaiah: “Lord, who has believed our message,
and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
NRSVCE.39: And so they could not believe, because Isaiah also said,
NRSVCE.40:
“He has blinded their eyes
and hardened their heart,
so that they might not look with their eyes,
and understand with their heart and turn —
and I would heal them.”
NRSVCE.41: Isaiah said this because he saw his glory and spoke about him.
NRSVCE.42: Nevertheless many, even of the authorities, believed in him. But because of the Pharisees they did not confess it, for fear that they would be put out of the synagogue;
NRSVCE.43: for they loved human glory more than the glory that comes from God.
NRSVCE.44: Then Jesus cried aloud: “Whoever believes in me believes not in me but in him who sent me.
NRSVCE.45: And whoever sees me sees him who sent me.
NRSVCE.46: I have come as light into the world, so that everyone who believes in me should not remain in the darkness.
NRSVCE.47: I do not judge anyone who hears my words and does not keep them, for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
NRSVCE.48: The one who rejects me and does not receive my word has a judge; on the last day the word that I have spoken will serve as judge,
NRSVCE.49: for I have not spoken on my own, but the Father who sent me has himself given me a commandment about what to say and what to speak.
NRSVCE.50: And I know that his commandment is eternal life. What I speak, therefore, I speak just as the Father has told me.”

NET.1: Then, six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus lived, whom he had raised from the dead.
NET.2: So they prepared a dinner for Jesus there. Martha was serving, and Lazarus was among those present at the table with him.
NET.3: Then Mary took three quarters of a pound of expensive aromatic oil from pure nard and anointed the feet of Jesus. She then wiped his feet dry with her hair. (Now the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfumed oil.)
NET.4: But Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples (the one who was going to betray him) said,
NET.5: “Why wasn’t this oil sold for three hundred silver coins and the money given to the poor?”
NET.6: (Now Judas said this not because he was concerned about the poor, but because he was a thief. As keeper of the money box, he used to steal what was put into it.)
NET.7: So Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She has kept it for the day of my burial.
NET.8: For you will always have the poor with you, but you will not always have me!”
NET.9: Now a large crowd of Judeans learned that Jesus was there, and so they came not only because of him but also to see Lazarus whom he had raised from the dead.
NET.10: So the chief priests planned to kill Lazarus too,
NET.11: for on account of him many of the Jewish people from Jerusalem were going away and believing in Jesus.
NET.12: The next day the large crowd that had come to the feast heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
NET.13: So they took branches of palm trees and went out to meet him. They began to shout, “Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessed is the king of Israel!”
NET.14: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written,
NET.15: “Do not be afraid, people of Zion; look, your king is coming, seated on a donkey’s colt!”
NET.16: (His disciples did not understand these things when they first happened, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written about him and that these things had happened to him.)
NET.17: So the crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were continuing to testify about it.
NET.18: Because they had heard that Jesus had performed this miraculous sign, the crowd went out to meet him.
NET.19: Thus the Pharisees said to one another, “You see that you can do nothing. Look, the world has run off after him!”
NET.20: Now some Greeks were among those who had gone up to worship at the feast.
NET.21: So these approached Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and requested, “Sir, we would like to see Jesus.”
NET.22: Philip went and told Andrew, and they both went and told Jesus.
NET.23: Jesus replied, “The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
NET.24: I tell you the solemn truth, unless a kernel of wheat falls into the ground and dies, it remains by itself alone. But if it dies, it produces much grain.
NET.25: The one who loves his life destroys it, and the one who hates his life in this world guards it for eternal life.
NET.26: If anyone wants to serve me, he must follow me, and where I am, my servant will be too. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.
NET.27: “Now my soul is greatly distressed. And what should I say? ‘Father, deliver me from this hour’? No, but for this very reason I have come to this hour.
NET.28: Father, glorify your name.” Then a voice came from heaven, “I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.”
NET.29: The crowd that stood there and heard the voice said that it had thundered. Others said that an angel had spoken to him.
NET.30: Jesus said, “This voice has not come for my benefit but for yours.
NET.31: Now is the judgment of this world; now the ruler of this world will be driven out.
NET.32: And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”
NET.33: (Now he said this to indicate clearly what kind of death he was going to die.)
NET.34: Then the crowd responded, “We have heard from the law that the Christ will remain forever. How can you say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this Son of Man?”
NET.35: Jesus replied, “The light is with you for a little while longer. Walk while you have the light, so that the darkness may not overtake you. The one who walks in the darkness does not know where he is going.
NET.36: While you have the light, believe in the light, so that you may become sons of light.” When Jesus had said these things, he went away and hid himself from them.
NET.37: Although Jesus had performed so many miraculous signs before them, they still refused to believe in him,
NET.38: so that the word of Isaiah the prophet would be fulfilled. He said, “Lord, who has believed our message, and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
NET.39: For this reason they could not believe, because again Isaiah said,
NET.40: “He has blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, so that they would not see with their eyes and understand with their heart, and turn to me, and I would heal them.”
NET.41: Isaiah said these things because he saw Christ’s glory, and spoke about him.
NET.42: Nevertheless, even among the rulers many believed in him, but because of the Pharisees they would not confess Jesus to be the Christ, so that they would not be put out of the synagogue.
NET.43: For they loved praise from men more than praise from God.
NET.44: But Jesus shouted out, “The one who believes in me does not believe in me, but in the one who sent me,
NET.45: and the one who sees me sees the one who sent me.
NET.46: I have come as a light into the world, so that everyone who believes in me should not remain in darkness.
NET.47: If anyone hears my words and does not obey them, I do not judge him. For I have not come to judge the world, but to save the world.
NET.48: The one who rejects me and does not accept my words has a judge; the word I have spoken will judge him at the last day.
NET.49: For I have not spoken from my own authority, but the Father himself who sent me has commanded me what I should say and what I should speak.
NET.50: And I know that his commandment is eternal life. Thus the things I say, I say just as the Father has told me.”
